key_insight: Machine learning potentials (MLP) enable efficient exploration of nitrogen-coordinated dual-atom catalysts for hydrogen evolution reaction.
engineering_relevance: The study presents a cost-effective approach to identify efficient catalysts for sustainable hydrogen production.
method: Screening of 23 transition metals across 20 nitrogen coordination motifs.
Utilization of a machine learning potential benchmarked against density functional theory.
Identification of synthesizable candidates with optimal binding characteristics.
evidence_signal: The MLP achieves near-DFT accuracy with a mean absolute error of 80 meV for Gibbs binding free energies.
watch_item: Further validation of MLP predictions with experimental results is necessary for practical implementation.
paper_title: Accelerated Discovery of Nitrogen-Coordinated Dual-Atom Hydrogen Evolution Reaction Electrocatalysts via Machine Learning Potentials
